destinations.evidence: Encrypted LLM output is decoded as a tuple whose second bytes field contains pricing data; empty pricing data is an error.
The pricing data itself is decoded as model, prompt token count, and completion token count, and invalid ABI data returns Err.

equivalence_argument: The port has an explicit encrypted-output branch that requires non-empty pricingData and decodes the exact pricing tuple before computing fees.
notes: Mapped to the port LLM pricing decoder.
rationale: The LLM decoder now has an explicit encrypted branch that extracts pricing bytes from ABI word 1 and refuses empty or undecodable pricing data before computing fees. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When encrypted-output mode is enabled for an LLM response, the executor output must decode as `(bytes encryptedData, bytes pricingData)`, where pricingData encodes `(string model, uint256 prompt_tokens, uint256 completion_tokens)`; empty or invalid pricingData must make LLM fee computation fail.
suggested_falsification_tests: Encode an encrypted LLM output with opaque encryptedData and pricingData containing model and token counts, then assert compute_llm_executor_fee succeeds and uses those metrics. Repeat with empty pricingData or malformed pricingData and assert fee computation returns an error.
summary: Encrypted LLM outputs carry separate pricingData that must decode to model and token counts.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Encode encryptedData with valid pricingData and assert fee computation succeeds; repeat with empty or malformed pricingData and assert Err.
title: Encrypted LLM outputs carry separate pricing data
